channel.title: Laurent DeSegur
channel.description: AI agents, deterministic runtimes, and the code that runs them. Building KarnEvil9, Tarkus, and LAID.

item.title: I Built a Chrome Extension That Detects AI-Generated LinkedIn Posts
item.description: 14 heuristic signals, peer-reviewed research, and an LLM rubric walk into your feed

item.title: Memory-Assisted Learning vs Recursive Language Models: An Empirical Study
item.description: Can an LLM improve at a game by reading its own past failures? A 72-game tic-tac-toe experiment using a swarm-based testbed shows hybrid memory+code beats pure prompt-recursive self-improvement.

item.title: Anthropic Just Validated What KarnEvil9 Has Been Building
item.description: Agent Skills are a step toward structured execution. KarnEvil9 is the full staircase.

item.title: The Real Frontier for Engineers
item.description: AI can recombine what already exists. The interesting work is the problems where no pattern exists yet.

item.title: Tarkus: a Native macOS Client for My AI Agent
item.description: Why I built a native macOS/iOS client for my autonomous AI agent instead of staring at terminal output

item.title: X/LAID v1.1: Now Detecting AI on Twitter Too
item.description: Dog-ear badges, a sensitivity curve that actually makes sense, and the leap from LinkedIn to X

item.title: When Eddie, My AI Agent, Ran Out of Credits and Invented Its Own Cost-Cutting Strategy
item.description: How KarnEvil9's futility monitor turned an API credit crisis into an autonomous cost-optimization strategy
